refusal_probes.question: Can I take my pet dog in the cabin with me?
refusal_probes.response: I don't have that information. I suggest contacting IndiGo support for details regarding traveling with pets in the cabin.

refusal_probes.question: How much does it cost to select a window seat in advance?
refusal_probes.response: I don't have that information. I suggest contacting IndiGo support for details on seat selection fees.

refusal_probes.question: Does IndiGo have a frequent flyer program and how do I earn points?
refusal_probes.response: I don't have that information. I suggest contacting IndiGo support for details regarding their frequent flyer program and how to earn points.

responses.question: How heavy can my cabin bag be?
responses.response: Your cabin bag can weigh a maximum of 7 kgs and must not exceed the dimensions of 55cm x 35cm x 25cm. Additionally, you are allowed to carry one personal article, such as a ladies' purse or a small bag containing a laptop, not weighing more than 3 kgs.

responses.question: What are the size limits for hand baggage?
responses.response: Each Customer is permitted to carry only 1 (one) Hand Baggage weighing a maximum of 7 (seven) kg, with dimensions not exceeding length 55 cm, width 35 cm, and height 25 cm. Additionally, Customers can carry one personal article not weighing more than 3 (three) kg.

responses.question: How much free check-in baggage do I get on a domestic flight?
responses.response: On a domestic flight with IndiGo, you get a free checked-in baggage allowance of 15 kg per customer, limited to 1 piece only. If you have a Double/MultiSeat booking, you will receive an extra 10 kg.

responses.question: I'm flying IndiGo business class - what's my baggage allowance?
responses.response: As an IndiGo business class customer, you are allowed free checked-in baggage up to 40 kg per customer, limited to 2 (two) pieces only. If your two pieces weigh above 40 kg, excess baggage charges will apply.

responses.question: Is there a maximum weight for a single suitcase?
responses.response: Yes, the maximum weight for a single suitcase is 23 kg. Heavier single pieces are not accepted.

responses.question: What is the baggage allowance for flights to Dubai?
responses.response: The baggage allowance for flights to Dubai is 30 kg per person, based on the sector. Additional excess baggage charges may apply.

responses.question: How much luggage can I take to Kathmandu?
responses.response: You can take a checked baggage allowance of 20 kg per person to Kathmandu. Additional excess baggage charges may apply if you exceed this limit.

responses.question: Do infants get a baggage allowance?
responses.response: Yes, infants are allowed one hand bag up to 7 kgs and 115 cms (L+W+H) per customer. Additionally, one stroller or baby pram per infant is allowed without any charge. There is no check-in baggage allowance for infants.

responses.question: How much do I pay per kg if my bag is overweight at the airport?
responses.response: If your bag is overweight at the airport, you will be charged at a rate of INR 700 per kg.

responses.question: What does 10kg of prepaid excess baggage cost for a domestic flight?
responses.response: For a domestic flight, 10 kg of prepaid excess baggage costs ₹ 6,500.

responses.question: What's the cheapest excess baggage option I can prebook?
responses.response: The cheapest excess baggage option you can pre-book is starting at ₹ 1,280. Additionally, for additional pieces of baggage, you can pre-book at a charge of ₹ 800 per piece, subject to a maximum of up to only 2 pieces.

responses.question: I booked a Lite fare - can I check in a bag?
responses.response: If you booked a Lite Fare, you may not be permitted to carry any Checked-in Baggage unless you pay the excess/additional Baggage charges. The charge for Lite Fare checked-in baggage is INR 1500, whether pre-booked or at the airport.

responses.question: How late can I buy prepaid excess baggage before my flight?
responses.response: You can book pre-paid excess baggage up to 1 hour prior to the scheduled departure of your domestic flight and 2 hours prior to the scheduled departure of your international flight. For international connecting flights, excess baggage should be booked at least 6 hours before the scheduled departure.

responses.question: What is the charge for carrying an extra piece of checked baggage?
responses.response: The charge for carrying an extra piece of checked baggage at the airport is INR 1000 per piece. If you pre-book the additional piece online, the charge is INR 800 per piece, subject to a maximum of 2 pieces.

responses.question: By when do I need to cancel my ticket if I don't want to lose the fare?
responses.response: You need to cancel your ticket at least 3 (three) hours prior to the scheduled departure time to avoid losing the fare. If you do not check-in for a flight at least 60 (sixty) minutes prior to its scheduled time of departure, the entire amount paid for the Booking will be retained by IndiGo.

responses.question: I booked yesterday directly on the IndiGo website. Can I still cancel for free?
responses.response: Yes, you can cancel for free within 48 hours from the time of booking, provided that your scheduled departure is not within 7 days for domestic flights. However, any fare difference between the original booking and the revised booking will still be payable by you.

responses.question: How long does a refund to my credit card take?
responses.response: For Bookings made through credit/debit cards or via wallets, the refund will be processed to the credit/debit card or to the wallet used to make such Booking within 7 (seven) working days from the date of the refund request.

responses.question: There's a spelling mistake in my name on the ticket. Can I fix it?
responses.response: Yes, you can correct a spelling mistake in your name within 24 (twenty-four) hours from the time of Booking without incurring any additional charges. You need to contact IndiGo's Customer Care number at +91 124-4973838 / 124-6173838 to make this correction, provided the ticket was booked directly on the IndiGo website or mobile application.

responses.question: What happens to my money if I miss my flight without cancelling?
responses.response: If you miss your flight without cancelling, IndiGo will retain the entire amount paid for the Booking. However, you are entitled to a refund of Airport Charges, excluding GST. For further assistance, you may want to contact IndiGo support.

responses.question: Is there an extra charge for cancelling through the call centre?
responses.response: Yes, if cancellations to Bookings are made through IndiGo's call centre, a facilitation fee of up to INR 500 per person per flight will be levied by IndiGo.

responses.question: What is a Credit Shell?
responses.response: A Credit Shell is a credit note issued by IndiGo to a Customer, which can be utilized for future travel on IndiGo flights. It is provided on account of any adjustment in the taxable value of services or compensation under applicable law, including redressal of Customer grievances as per the Conditions of Carriage.

responses.question: How early should I reach the airport before my IndiGo flight?
responses.response: IndiGo recommends that Customers report for check-in at IndiGo counters at least 2 (two) hours prior to the departure of the scheduled flight. Additionally, Customers are required to check-in their baggage/bag drop at least 60 (sixty) minutes prior to their departure time.

responses.question: Until when can I do web check-in?
responses.response: You can do web check-in on the IndiGo website anytime up to one (1) hour prior to the scheduled departure of your flight.

responses.question: Can I use my Aadhaar card as ID at check-in?
responses.response: Yes, you can use your Aadhaar or m-Aadhaar issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India ("UIDAI") as a valid identification document at check-in.

responses.question: Who is not allowed to sit in the emergency exit row?
responses.response: Customers who are not allowed to sit in the emergency exit row include those who are not able-bodied, are under 15 years of age on the date of travel, have a physical or mental condition that prevents them from opening an emergency exit, are suffering from a temporary illness affecting their mobility, are traveling with an infant, or are unwilling to assist in an emergency.

responses.question: My flight was overbooked and I was denied boarding. What am I entitled to?
responses.response: If you were denied boarding due to an overbooked flight, IndiGo will endeavor to seek volunteers to surrender their bookings. If you are not a volunteer and are denied boarding, you may be compensated in the form of vouchers, unless IndiGo arranges a seat on an alternate flight scheduled to depart within 1 (one) hour of your original flight. For more specific details regarding your situation, please contact IndiGo support.

responses.question: Can I carry a power bank in my checked luggage?
responses.response: No, power banks and spare/loose lithium batteries are permitted only in hand baggage. They must remain within the passenger's personal reach at all times and must not be stowed in overhead compartments or checked baggage.

responses.question: How much alcohol can I carry in my checked bag?
responses.response: You can carry up to 5 litres of alcoholic beverages as part of your checked-in baggage, provided the alcoholic beverage is in retail packaging and packed appropriately to prevent damage or leakage. The alcohol content must not exceed 70%. If the beverage contains 24% or less alcohol by volume, the 5-litre limitation does not apply.

responses.question: Can I bring my hoverboard on the flight?
responses.response: No, battery-operated hoverboards are not permitted as baggage on IndiGo flights. For more information, you may contact IndiGo support.

responses.question: I want to carry a big LED TV back home. Is that allowed?
responses.response: Yes, you can carry a maximum of two (02) LCD/LED TVs in Checked-in Baggage, provided the maximum size of each TV does not exceed 139.7 centimetres including the cartons. However, you must notify IndiGo at least 48 (forty-eight) hours in advance through the call centre if you intend to carry a TV as Checked-in Baggage.

responses.question: Are e-cigarettes allowed on IndiGo flights?
responses.response: No, e-cigarettes are not permitted on IndiGo flights. Customers are not allowed to carry e-cigarettes in their hand baggage or checked-in baggage, and IndiGo reserves the right to refuse carriage of customers in possession of e-cigarettes.

responses.question: My checked bag was lost by the airline. How much compensation can I get?
responses.response: For lost Checked-in Baggage, IndiGo's liability is limited to INR 350 per kg, subject to a maximum of INR 25,000 per passenger, unless a special declaration is made at the time of Check-in.

responses.question: My checked bag is at 13kg and I want to add a wine bottle. Domestic flight - will I go over the limit, and if yes what do I pay?
responses.response: Yes, you will exceed the limit. The checked baggage allowance for domestic flights is 15 kg per passenger. If you add a wine bottle, you will be over the limit, and excess baggage charges of INR 700 per kg will apply for any weight exceeding 15 kg.

responses.question: I'm travelling with a 28kg checked bag. Does it matter whether my flight is to Dubai or to Nairobi?
responses.response: Yes, it matters. For a flight to Dubai, the free baggage allowance is 30 kg per person, while for a flight to Nairobi, the allowance is 25 kg per person. Since your checked bag is 28 kg, it would be acceptable for the Dubai flight but would incur excess baggage charges for the Nairobi flight.

responses.question: I have two bags for my domestic trip and the second one counts as an extra piece. Do I save money by booking it online instead of paying at the airport?
responses.response: Yes, you can save money by booking your additional piece of baggage online. The pre-booking charge for an additional piece is INR 800 per piece, while the charge at the airport is INR 1000 per piece. Therefore, booking online is more cost-effective.

responses.question: How much will 10kg of extra baggage cost me?
responses.response: The cost for 10 kg of extra baggage when pre-booked is ₹ 6,500. If you choose to pay at the airport, the charge is ₹ 7,000 per kg.
